summary: Iteration 2: Drawer NavigationContainer crash from iteration 1 is RESOLVED. Full mobile UI flow verified end-to-end at 390x844. All requested testIDs render and interactions work: sign-in -> AI Interface, AI chat (user bubble -> typing-indicator -> AI bubble), drawer with 4 destinations, dashboard cards (temperature/ph/food), camera (frame/bounding-box/fish-label/capture), products grid (8 cards, 6 filter chips including All/Equipment/Sensor/Food/Decor, reorder button shows 'Reordered'), and sign-out returning to sign-in. No red screens. No page errors. No console errors observed.
tests_passed: sign-in with aquarist@test.com/password123 redirects to AI Interface (drawer landing) - ai-header visible
open-drawer-button opens drawer; drawer-ai/dashboard/camera/products and signout-button + drawer-username present
AI chat: chat-input + send-button -> chat-bubble-user -> typing-indicator -> chat-bubble-ai (canned)
Dashboard: temperature-card, ph-card, food-card all render with values (24 C, 7.2 pH, 85%)
Camera: camera-frame, bounding-box, fish-label ('Fish Type: Goldfish 92%'), capture-button tap shows 'Captured' confirmation; placeholder gradient as expected on web
Products: 8 visible product-card-*, 6 visible filter-chip-* (All/Equipment/Sensor/Food/Decor/+1), 8 reorder-button-* visible; tapping a reorder button updates label to 'Reordered'
signout-button returns to sign-in screen (signin-button visible)
critical_code_review_comments: Multiple drawer screens are kept mounted simultaneously, so each has its own open-drawer-button in the DOM. UI works because only the active screen is visible, but automated tests must pick the visible instance (not the first match). Not a bug, just a test-author note for future agents.
authStore is in-memory only; reload returns to sign-in (matches PRD).
All interactive elements have testID attributes that propagate correctly to web (data-testid).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use https://aqua-interface-dev.preview.emergentagent.com on viewport 390x844. Sign in with aquarist@test.com / password123. IMPORTANT: open-drawer-button exists once per drawer screen (all kept mounted). When clicking it, iterate over locator matches and click the first VISIBLE one (or use page.locator('[data-testid=open-drawer-button]:visible')).
